description:
This conceptual diagram illustrates land cover changes between 2012 and 2018 and how they are processed for the Riparian Zones (RZ) product, considering a Minimum Mapping Unit (MMU) of 0.5 ha.

The sequence shows four panels:
1.  **IMAGE 2012**: Displays a uniform red area classified as 1.1.1.
2.  **IMAGE 2018**: Shows land cover changes. A part of the original 1.1.1 area is now occupied by two new land cover classes: 1.3.1 (brown, 0.8 ha) and 8.x.x (blue, 0.3 ha). The remaining area is still 1.1.1.
3.  **RZ 2012**: Depicts the Riparian Zones product for 2012. A central square area within the 1.1.1 class is outlined in black, indicating the mapped Riparian Zone object for that year.
4.  **RZ 2018**: Depicts the Riparian Zones product for 2018. Only the 1.3.1 land cover class (brown, 0.8 ha) is shown as a mapped Riparian Zone object. The 8.x.x land cover class (blue, 0.3 ha) is not included in the RZ 2018 product because its area is below the 0.5 ha Minimum Mapping Unit.

The diagram demonstrates that while both 1.3.1 and 8.x.x represent changes in the raw image, only the polygon exceeding or meeting the 0.5 ha MMU (1.3.1 at 0.8 ha) is incorporated into the final Riparian Zones product.
